name: Dressrosa
aliases: Country of Love, Passion and Toys
description: Dressrosa is a tropical island kingdom known for its passionate citizens, flamenco dancing, and living toys that coexist with humans. Beneath its colorful surface lies a dark history of manipulation and forgotten memories, orchestrated by the former Warlord Donquixote Doflamingo.
history: Dressrosa was originally ruled by the Riku royal family for generations as a peaceful kingdom. 900 years ago, the Donquixote Family were its rulers before becoming World Nobles. Doflamingo returned ten years before the main story and used his powers to frame King Riku, taking over the kingdom. He used Sugar's Devil Fruit power to turn dissenters into toys, erasing them from memory. The underground port managed the illegal SMILE fruit trade and an arena for gladiator battles. The country was eventually liberated when Luffy defeated Doflamingo, and King Riku was restored to power.
culture: Dressrosan culture is vibrant and passionate, with flamenco dancing, colorful architecture, and festivals being central to life. Citizens are known for their fiery temperament and romantic nature. The coexistence with living toys was normalized through Sugar's powers. The Corrida Colosseum gladiator tournaments are major entertainment events. Art, music, and dance permeate daily life.
transportation: Citizens walk through the cobblestone streets, use horse-drawn carriages, and ride on special Fighting Fish-pulled boats in the waterways. The toy trains and cable cars connect different areas of the island.

quirks: Spicy dishes reflecting the passionate culture

notablePeople.name: Donquixote Doflamingo
notablePeople.role: Former King / Former Warlord / Underground Broker (Joker)
notablePeople.description: The manipulative tyrant who ruled Dressrosa through fear and memory manipulation. User of the String-String Fruit.

notablePeople.name: King Riku Doldo III
notablePeople.description: The beloved former king who was framed by Doflamingo. Known for his kindness and dedication to his people.

notablePeople.name: Rebecca
notablePeople.description: King Riku's granddaughter who fought in the Corrida Colosseum to win back the Mera Mera no Mi.

notablePeople.name: Kyros
notablePeople.description: The undefeated gladiator turned into a toy soldier, Rebecca's father who fought for Dressrosa's freedom.

notablePeople.name: Viola (Violet)
notablePeople.description: King Riku's daughter who pretended to serve Doflamingo to protect her father. Ate the Glare-Glare Fruit, granting clairvoyance.

notablePeople.name: Trafalgar Law
notablePeople.role: Pirate Captain / Warlord / Straw Hat Ally
notablePeople.description: Formed an alliance with Luffy to take down Doflamingo as part of his revenge against the man who killed his benefactor.

notablePeople.name: Sabo
notablePeople.description: Luffy's sworn brother who competed for and won the Mera Mera no Mi at the Corrida Colosseum.

notablePeople.name: Bartolomeo
notablePeople.description: Captain of the Barto Club and massive Straw Hat fanboy. Became captain of the 2nd division of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.

notablePeople.name: Cavendish
notablePeople.description: The narcissistic 'Beautiful Pirate Prince' with a split personality - Hakuba. Captain of the 1st division of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.

notablePeople.name: Sugar
notablePeople.description: The child-like officer whose Hobby-Hobby Fruit powers enabled Doflamingo's entire scheme.

notablePeople.name: Diamante
notablePeople.description: One of Doflamingo's three elite officers. Killed Rebecca's mother years ago. Wields the Flag-Flag Fruit.

notablePeople.name: Trebol
notablePeople.description: One of Doflamingo's original crew who gave him his Devil Fruit as a child. Wields the Sticky-Sticky Fruit.

notablePeople.name: Pica
notablePeople.description: One of Doflamingo's three elite officers with a comically high-pitched voice. Ate the Stone-Stone Fruit. Defeated by Zoro.

notablePeople.name: Leo
notablePeople.description: Captain of the Tontatta warriors and wielder of the Stitch-Stitch Fruit. Led the dwarf rebellion and became the 5th division commander of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.

notablePeople.name: Mansherry
notablePeople.description: Princess of the Tontatta Tribe with the Heal-Heal Fruit power. Was held captive by Doflamingo to exploit her healing abilities.

notablePeople.name: Fujitora (Issho)
notablePeople.description: A blind Admiral with the Gravity-Gravity Fruit. Despite being ordered to capture Luffy, he respects him and intentionally let him escape.

notablePeople.name: Senor Pink
notablePeople.description: A 'hardboiled' assassin who dresses like a baby due to a tragic past. Wields the Swim-Swim Fruit. Fought Franky.

notablePeople.name: Bellamy
notablePeople.description: Former enemy from Mock Town who idolized Doflamingo. Betrayed by Doflamingo and saved by Luffy, leading to his redemption.

subLocations.name: Corrida Colosseum
subLocations.description: A massive gladiatorial arena where warriors compete for prizes, including Devil Fruits. The site of the tournament for Ace's Flame-Flame Fruit that drew fighters from across the world.

subLocations.name: Flower Hill
subLocations.description: A beautiful hill covered in sunflowers where Kyros lived as the Thunder Soldier with Rebecca. Now a memorial site for Scarlett and a symbol of hope.

subLocations.name: SMILE Factory (Destroyed)
subLocations.description: The massive underground factory where artificial Devil Fruits (SMILEs) were manufactured for Kaido. Powered by enslaved Tontatta dwarves. Destroyed during the final battle.

subLocations.name: Royal Palace
subLocations.description: The seat of power in Dressrosa. Was occupied by Doflamingo for 10 years before being restored to the Riku Family. Site of the final battle between Luffy and Doflamingo.
notableFeatures: Battle damage from Luffy vs Doflamingo

subLocations.name: Green Bit
subLocations.description: A small island connected to Dressrosa by an iron bridge, home to the Tontatta Tribe. Contains the ancient ruins of the Donquixote Family's original kingdom.

subLocations.name: Toy House
subLocations.description: Where the 'living toys' worked and resided, their existence normalized by Sugar's powers. After her defeat, it became a memorial to the horror of forgotten people.

subLocations.name: Acacia Harbor
subLocations.description: The main port of Dressrosa where ships dock. Features the iron bridge to Green Bit and is patrolled by Fighting Fish.

subLocations.name: Primula District
subLocations.description: A residential district of Dressrosa with Spanish-style architecture, flower-lined streets, and passionate citizens.

items.name: Mera Mera no Mi (Flame-Flame Fruit)
items.description: Ace's former Devil Fruit that reappeared after his death. Was the grand prize at the Corrida Colosseum tournament. Now possessed by Sabo.

items.name: SMILE Artificial Devil Fruit
items.description: Defective artificial Devil Fruits manufactured in the factory. Only 10% grant actual Zoan powers; the rest cause permanent smiling. Production stopped.

items.name: Birdcage
items.description: Doflamingo's ultimate technique using his String-String Fruit - a massive cage of strings that can shrink and slice through anything, trapping an entire country.

items.name: Explosive Collar
items.description: Explosive devices Doflamingo placed on key figures to ensure loyalty. Would detonate if they disobeyed or tried to escape.

items.name: Eternal Pose to Zou
items.description: A permanent Log Pose pointing to the moving elephant island of Zou, given to the Straw Hats.

items.name: Kyros' Bronze Statue
items.description: A statue of the legendary gladiator that was mysteriously one-legged. After Sugar's defeat, people remembered it was actually Kyros himself turned to bronze by her powers.

items.name: Tontatta Growth Accelerator
items.description: Special dwarven technique that makes crops grow incredibly fast with amazing quality. Used to cultivate the grapes for SMILE fruits.

items.name: Fighting Fish
items.description: Aggressive bull-like fish that patrol the waters around Dressrosa and pull barges to Green Bit.

factions.name: Riku Family (Restored)
factions.description: The rightful royal family of Dressrosa, known for their benevolence and wisdom. Restored to power after Doflamingo's defeat.

factions.name: Donquixote Pirates (Defeated)
factions.description: A powerful criminal organization led by a former Celestial Dragon. Controlled the underworld arms trade, SMILE production, and ruled Dressrosa through terror. Completely dismantled.

factions.name: Straw Hat Grand Fleet
factions.description: Seven pirate crews totaling 5,640 members who swore allegiance to Luffy after he saved Dressrosa. Formed despite Luffy's protests.

factions.name: Tontatta Tribe
factions.description: The dwarf tribe living in Green Bit, known for extreme gullibility but incredible strength and speed. Were enslaved to work in the SMILE Factory but liberated.

factions.name: Revolutionary Army (Dressrosa Unit)
factions.description: Revolutionary Army members who came to Dressrosa to investigate Doflamingo's weapons trade and support the rebellion.

factions.name: Marines (Dressrosa Occupation)
factions.description: Marine forces sent to arrest Luffy and Law but ultimately helped clean up after Doflamingo's defeat. Fujitora apologized to King Riku for the World Government's failure.

events: Corrida Colosseum tournament for the Mera Mera no Mi
Sugar's defeat and return of memories
Formation of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet

quickFacts: Famous for living toys walking among citizens
Home of the Corrida Colosseum gladiator arena
Where the Straw Hat Grand Fleet was formed
Underground SMILE factory connected to Kaido
Citizens are known for passionate personalities

videos.title: Luffy vs Doflamingo - King Kong Gun
videos.description: The climactic final blow that freed Dressrosa from tyranny.

videos.title: Sugar Defeated - Toys Return to Normal
videos.description: The emotional moment when forgotten loved ones are remembered again.

videos.title: Sabo Returns and Gets the Mera Mera no Mi
videos.description: Luffy's brother returns and inherits Ace's flame powers.
